rulings.Case Title: TYEE KYVELOS VS. ADRIAN HOTEL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for December 17, 2025, line 1. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF TYEE KYVELOS'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Transferred to be heard in department 501 on the same date at 9:30 a.m. per SF Local Rule 8.10A1. That department handles all nuisance law and motion matters. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/VJ-CM)

rulings.Case Title: GREGORY OVALLE VS. ACQUITY REALITY INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents From Acquity Realty Inc. Ccp 2031.300
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for December 17, 2025, line 2. PLAINTIFF GREGORY OVALLE'S Motion To Compel Production Of Documents From Acquity Realty Inc. CCP 2031.300. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Production of Documents by Defendant Acquity Realty Inc. is continued on the court's own motion to December 23, 2025. The Declaration of Ronald K. Sittler in support of the motion refers to exhibits which have not been submitted in support of the motion. To have the merits of the motion considered on December 23, 2025, Plaintiff must promptly serve and file an amended declaration with the exhibits attached. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: KEEP AMERICA SAFE AND BEAUTIFUL VS. GAMEBREAKER, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for December 17, 2025, line 3. PLAINTIFF KEEP AMERICA SAFE AND BEAUTIFUL'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And Consent Judgment. Plaintiff's Motion to Approve Proposition 65 Settlement and Consent Judgment is granted. The documents submitted in support of Plaintiff's unopposed motion establish: (1) the injunctive relief required by the settlement complies with Proposition 65; (2) the civil penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria in Health and Safety Code 25249.7(b); and (3) the attorney's fees and costs to be awarded by stipulation are reasonable under California law. The court will sign the stipulated Consent Judgement and maintain jurisdiction to enforce the judgment pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 664.6. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/VJ-CM)
